---
title: 'STA 035C Homework 1: R Part'
author: "Yuan Xie"
date: "2025-04-10"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE,eval = TRUE)
```

## Import Environment and Dataset

```{r}
# import the tidyverse environment
library(tidyverse)
```

---

## Problem 2
### (c)
```{r}
# set the values of theta
theta_vals <- c(0.2, 0.5, 0.8)
# repeat 100 times
n <- 100
# set the true p value at 0.3 at this time
p_true1 <- 0.3

# set the seed to keep reproducibility
set.seed(123)
# the results of flips are in a binomial distribution
observed_flips <- rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = p_true1)

# initialize the priors
prior <- rep(1/3, 3)  # 3 thetas
posterior_history <- matrix(NA, nrow = n, ncol = 3)

# update it gradually
for (i in 1:n) {
  x <- observed_flips[i]
  # Likelihood
  likelihood <- if (x == 1) theta_vals else (1 - theta_vals)
  # Bayes rule
  unnorm_posterior <- likelihood * prior
  posterior <- unnorm_posterior / sum(unnorm_posterior)
  # record
  posterior_history[i, ] <- posterior
  # next prior = current posterior
  prior <- posterior
}

# transfer the recoreded data into a data frame
df1 <- as.data.frame(posterior_history)
colnames(df1) <- paste0("theta_", theta_vals)
df1$step <- 1:n
df1_long <- pivot_longer(df1, cols = starts_with("theta_"), names_to = "theta", values_to = "posterior")

# plot the graph
ggplot(df1_long, aes(x = step, y = posterior, color = theta)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = paste("Posterior update over time (p_true =", p_true1, ")"),
       x = "Number of Flips",
       y = "Posterior Probability",
       color = expression(theta))








# set the values of theta
theta_vals <- c(0.2, 0.5, 0.8)
# repeat 100 times
n <- 100
# set the true p value at 0.5 at this time
p_true2 <- 0.5

# set the seed to keep reproducibility
set.seed(123)
# the results of flips are in a binomial distribution
observed_flips <- rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = p_true2)

# initialize the priors
prior <- rep(1/3, 3)  # 3 thetas
posterior_history <- matrix(NA, nrow = n, ncol = 3)

# update it gradually
for (i in 1:n) {
  x <- observed_flips[i]
  # Likelihood
  likelihood <- if (x == 1) theta_vals else (1 - theta_vals)
  # Bayes rule
  unnorm_posterior <- likelihood * prior
  posterior <- unnorm_posterior / sum(unnorm_posterior)
  # record
  posterior_history[i, ] <- posterior
  # next prior = current posterior
  prior <- posterior
}

# transfer the recoreded data into a data frame
df2 <- as.data.frame(posterior_history)
colnames(df2) <- paste0("theta_", theta_vals)
df2$step <- 1:n
df2_long <- pivot_longer(df2, cols = starts_with("theta_"), names_to = "theta", values_to = "posterior")

# plot the graph
ggplot(df2_long, aes(x = step, y = posterior, color = theta)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = paste("Posterior update over time (p_true =", p_true2, ")"),
       x = "Number of Flips",
       y = "Posterior Probability",
       color = expression(theta))
```

### (d)
```{r}
# No idea temporarily
```

## Problem 3
### (c)
```{r}
# define a function for the cases n = 100 and n = 1000
run_simulation <- function(n) {
  # keep the result reproducible
  set.seed(123) 
  
  # generate the data based on the uniform distribution of x and normal distribution of error
  X <- runif(n, min = 0, max = 10)
  # the epsilon, error is in the normal distribution
  epsilon <- rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = 2)
  # this is the linear model of the regression line
  Y <- 2 + 3 * X + epsilon
  
  # draw the best fit line based on the sample points
  model <- lm(Y ~ X)
  # summarize the important parameters
  summary_model <- summary(model)
  
  print(paste("n =", n))
  
  # print the summarized coefficients
  print("(ii) Coefficients:")
  print(coef(summary_model))
  
  # get the residual standard error
  rse <- summary_model$sigma
  print("(iii) Residual Standard Error:")
  print(rse)
  print("True sigma: 2")
  
  # set a 95% confidence interval
  print("(iv) 95% Confidence Intervals:")
  print(confint(model))
  
  # make a hypothesis test. H0: beta_1 = 0
  t_stat <- coef(summary_model)["X", "t value"]
  p_val <- coef(summary_model)["X", "Pr(>|t|)"]
  print("(v) t-statistic and p-value:")
  print(paste("t =", round(t_stat, 4)))
  print(paste("p =", round(p_val, 4)))
  
  if (p_val < 0.05) {
    print("Reject H0: beta_1 is significantly different from 0")
  } else {
    print("Fail to reject H0: beta_1 is not significantly different from 0")
  }
  
  # calculate the R squared
  r_squared <- summary_model$r.squared
  print("(vi) R-squared:")
  print(r_squared)
  print(paste("Interpretation: ", round(r_squared * 100, 2), "% of the variance in Y is explained by X."))
}

# execute n = 100 and n = 1000 times
run_simulation(100)
run_simulation(1000)
```

### (d)
```{r}
# define a function for the cases n = 100 and n = 1000
run_quadratic_simulation <- function(n) {
  # keep the result reproducible
  set.seed(123) 
  
  # generate the data based on the uniform distribution of x and normal distribution of error
  X <- runif(n, min = 0, max = 10)
  epsilon <- rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = 2)
  
  # the mathematical model is quadratic
  Y <- 2 + 3 * X^2 + epsilon
  # the required fitted model is still a linear model
  model <- lm(Y ~ X)
  # summarize the important parameters
  summary_model <- summary(model)
  
  print(paste("n =", n, "(quadratic data, linear fit)"))
  
  # print the summarized coefficients
  print("(ii) Coefficients:")
  print(coef(summary_model))
  
  # get the residual standard error
  rse <- summary_model$sigma
  print("(iii) Residual Standard Error:")
  print(rse)
  print("True sigma: 2")
  
  # set a 95% confidence interval
  print("(iv) 95% Confidence Intervals:")
  print(confint(model))
  
  # make a hypothesis test. H0: beta_1 = 0
  t_stat <- coef(summary_model)["X", "t value"]
  p_val <- coef(summary_model)["X", "Pr(>|t|)"]
  print("(v) t-statistic and p-value:")
  print(paste("t =", round(t_stat, 4)))
  print(paste("p =", round(p_val, 4)))
  
  if (p_val < 0.05) {
    print("Reject H0: beta_1 is significantly different from 0")
  } else {
    print("Fail to reject H0: beta_1 is not significantly different from 0")
  }
  
  # calculate the R squared
  r_squared <- summary_model$r.squared
  print("(vi) R-squared:")
  print(r_squared)
  print(paste("Interpretation: ", round(r_squared * 100, 2), "% of the variance in Y is (wrongly) explained by X."))
}

# execute n = 100 and n = 1000 times
run_quadratic_simulation(100)
run_quadratic_simulation(1000)
```

## Problem 4
### (a)
```{r}
# keep the result reproducible
set.seed(123)

# Simulate data: true relationship is linear
n <- 100
X <- runif(n, 0, 10)  # X ~ Uniform(0, 10)
epsilon <- rnorm(n, 0, 2)  # error = epsilon ~ N(0, 2^2)
Y <- 5 + 2 * X + epsilon  # true model: linear

# Fit a linear regression model
model_linear <- lm(Y ~ X)
rss_linear <- sum(resid(model_linear)^2)  # Residual Sum of Squares (RSS)

# Fit a cubic regression model (adds X^2 and X^3 terms)
model_cubic <- lm(Y ~ X + I(X^2) + I(X^3))
rss_cubic <- sum(resid(model_cubic)^2)

# Print RSS results
print(paste("Linear model RSS:", round(rss_linear, 4)))
print(paste("Cubic model RSS:", round(rss_cubic, 4)))
```

### (b)
```{r}
# keep the result reproducible
set.seed(123)

# training the linear model with 100 points
n_train <- 100
X_train <- runif(n_train, 0, 10)
epsilon_train <- rnorm(n_train, 0, 2)
Y_train <- 5 + 2 * X_train + epsilon_train  # true linear model

# Generate test data (unknown by model)
n_test <- 1000
X_test <- runif(n_test, 0, 10)
epsilon_test <- rnorm(n_test, 0, 2)
Y_test <- 5 + 2 * X_test + epsilon_test

# Fit models on both the two training data
model_linear <- lm(Y_train ~ X_train)
model_cubic <- lm(Y_train ~ X_train + I(X_train^2) + I(X_train^3))

# Predict on test data, both linear and cubic
pred_linear <- predict(model_linear, newdata = data.frame(X_train = X_test))
pred_cubic <- predict(model_cubic, newdata = data.frame(X_train = X_test))

# Compute test RSS
rss_linear_test <- sum((Y_test - pred_linear)^2)
rss_cubic_test <- sum((Y_test - pred_cubic)^2)

# Print test RSS
print(paste("Linear model test RSS:", round(rss_linear_test, 2)))
print(paste("Cubic model test RSS:", round(rss_cubic_test, 2)))
```

### (c)
```{r}
# ----- (i) Linear data -----
set.seed(123)

# Generate training data
n_train <- 100
X_train <- runif(n_train, 0, 10)
epsilon_train <- rnorm(n_train, 0, 2)
Y_train <- 5 + 2 * X_train + epsilon_train  # true linear model

# Generate test data
n_test <- 1000
X_test <- runif(n_test, 0, 10)
epsilon_test <- rnorm(n_test, 0, 2)
Y_test <- 5 + 2 * X_test + epsilon_test

# Fit linear and cubic models
model_linear <- lm(Y_train ~ X_train)
model_cubic <- lm(Y_train ~ X_train + I(X_train^2) + I(X_train^3))

# Predict on training data
pred_train_linear <- predict(model_linear)
pred_train_cubic <- predict(model_cubic)

# Predict on test data
pred_test_linear <- predict(model_linear, newdata = data.frame(X_train = X_test))
pred_test_cubic <- predict(model_cubic, newdata = data.frame(X_train = X_test))

# Compute training RSS
rss_train_linear <- sum((Y_train - pred_train_linear)^2)
rss_train_cubic <- sum((Y_train - pred_train_cubic)^2)

# Compute test RSS
rss_test_linear <- sum((Y_test - pred_test_linear)^2)
rss_test_cubic <- sum((Y_test - pred_test_cubic)^2)

# Print results
print("(i) Linear Data, n = 100")
print(paste("Training RSS - Linear:", round(rss_train_linear, 2)))
print(paste("Training RSS - Cubic :", round(rss_train_cubic, 2)))
print(paste("Test RSS - Linear:", round(rss_test_linear, 2)))
print(paste("Test RSS - Cubic :", round(rss_test_cubic, 2)))





# ----- (ii) Nonlinear data -----
set.seed(123)

# Generate training data
X_train <- runif(n_train, 0, 10)
epsilon_train <- rnorm(n_train, 0, 2)
Y_train <- 0.5 * X_train^2 + 2 * sin(X_train) + epsilon_train

# Generate test data
X_test <- runif(n_test, 0, 10)
epsilon_test <- rnorm(n_test, 0, 2)
Y_test <- 0.5 * X_test^2 + 2 * sin(X_test) + epsilon_test

# Fit models
model_linear <- lm(Y_train ~ X_train)
model_cubic <- lm(Y_train ~ X_train + I(X_train^2) + I(X_train^3))

# Predict
pred_train_linear <- predict(model_linear)
pred_train_cubic <- predict(model_cubic)
pred_test_linear <- predict(model_linear, newdata = data.frame(X_train = X_test))
pred_test_cubic <- predict(model_cubic, newdata = data.frame(X_train = X_test))

# RSS
rss_train_linear <- sum((Y_train - pred_train_linear)^2)
rss_train_cubic <- sum((Y_train - pred_train_cubic)^2)
rss_test_linear <- sum((Y_test - pred_test_linear)^2)
rss_test_cubic <- sum((Y_test - pred_test_cubic)^2)

# Print
print("(ii) Nonlinear Data, n = 100")
print(paste("Training RSS - Linear:", round(rss_train_linear, 2)))
print(paste("Training RSS - Cubic :", round(rss_train_cubic, 2)))
print(paste("Test RSS - Linear:", round(rss_test_linear, 2)))
print(paste("Test RSS - Cubic :", round(rss_test_cubic, 2)))






# ----- (iii) Linear Data, n = 10 -----
set.seed(123)

# Generate training data
n_train <- 10
X_train <- runif(n_train, 0, 10)
epsilon_train <- rnorm(n_train, 0, 2)
Y_train <- 5 + 2 * X_train + epsilon_train  # True linear model

# Generate test data
n_test <- 1000
X_test <- runif(n_test, 0, 10)
epsilon_test <- rnorm(n_test, 0, 2)
Y_test <- 5 + 2 * X_test + epsilon_test

# Fit models
model_linear <- lm(Y_train ~ X_train)
model_cubic <- lm(Y_train ~ X_train + I(X_train^2) + I(X_train^3))

# Predict on training and test data
pred_train_linear <- predict(model_linear)
pred_train_cubic <- predict(model_cubic)
pred_test_linear <- predict(model_linear, newdata = data.frame(X_train = X_test))
pred_test_cubic <- predict(model_cubic, newdata = data.frame(X_train = X_test))

# Compute RSS
rss_train_linear <- sum((Y_train - pred_train_linear)^2)
rss_train_cubic <- sum((Y_train - pred_train_cubic)^2)
rss_test_linear <- sum((Y_test - pred_test_linear)^2)
rss_test_cubic <- sum((Y_test - pred_test_cubic)^2)

# Print results
print("(iii) Linear Data, n = 10")
print(paste("Training RSS - Linear:", round(rss_train_linear, 2)))
print(paste("Training RSS - Cubic :", round(rss_train_cubic, 2)))
print(paste("Test RSS - Linear:", round(rss_test_linear, 2)))
print(paste("Test RSS - Cubic :", round(rss_test_cubic, 2)))





# ----- (iv) Nonlinear Data, n = 10 -----
set.seed(123)

# Generate training data
n_train <- 10
X_train <- runif(n_train, 0, 10)
epsilon_train <- rnorm(n_train, 0, 2)
Y_train <- 0.5 * X_train^2 + 2 * sin(X_train) + epsilon_train  # True nonlinear model

# Generate test data
n_test <- 1000
X_test <- runif(n_test, 0, 10)
epsilon_test <- rnorm(n_test, 0, 2)
Y_test <- 0.5 * X_test^2 + 2 * sin(X_test) + epsilon_test

# Fit models
model_linear <- lm(Y_train ~ X_train)
model_cubic <- lm(Y_train ~ X_train + I(X_train^2) + I(X_train^3))

# Predict on training and test data
pred_train_linear <- predict(model_linear)
pred_train_cubic <- predict(model_cubic)
pred_test_linear <- predict(model_linear, newdata = data.frame(X_train = X_test))
pred_test_cubic <- predict(model_cubic, newdata = data.frame(X_train = X_test))

# Compute RSS
rss_train_linear <- sum((Y_train - pred_train_linear)^2)
rss_train_cubic <- sum((Y_train - pred_train_cubic)^2)
rss_test_linear <- sum((Y_test - pred_test_linear)^2)
rss_test_cubic <- sum((Y_test - pred_test_cubic)^2)

# Print results
print("(iv) Nonlinear Data, n = 10")
print(paste("Training RSS - Linear:", round(rss_train_linear, 2)))
print(paste("Training RSS - Cubic :", round(rss_train_cubic, 2)))
print(paste("Test RSS - Linear:", round(rss_test_linear, 2)))
print(paste("Test RSS - Cubic :", round(rss_test_cubic, 2)))
```
